His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region.In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction.Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively.As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ AUDDIA AND THRAMANN MERGE TO ESTABLISH MCCARTHY FINNEY HOLDING

Auddia Inc. has announced a definitive merger agreement with Thramann Holdings to form a new Delaware holding company named McCarthy Finney, Inc. The announcement was made on 17th February 2026, and the details of the merger were disclosed on 18th February 2026. Under this agreement, both Auddia and Thramann will become wholly owned subsidiaries of McCarthy Finney, with Auddia shareholders receiving one common share of McCarthy Finney for each share they hold in Auddia, while Thramann owners will receive a combination of special preferred stock and $3.5 million in unsecured notes.Post-merger, Auddia shareholders are projected to own approximately 20% of McCarthy Finney, while former Thramann stakeholders are expected to hold around 80%, contingent on Auddia maintaining a minimum cash balance of $12 million. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann will carry significant governance rights, including a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million, alongside flexible conversion and redemption features, giving Thramann substantial influence over the newly formed entity.Both Auddia and Thramann's boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness, supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is assured, with Auddia's four directors set to join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO, respectively. Additionally, Auddia's executives and directors have committed to support the deal, and Thramann will adhere to a 180-day lock-up on his shares in McCarthy Finney.In terms of valuation, Auddia estimates a base-case worth of $250 million for McCarthy Finney, based on a management-prepared discounted cash flow analysis of the combined entity's pro forma over the next decade. The merger is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and meeting Nasdaq listing requirements. The formation of McCarthy Finney positions the company as a diversified holding entity focused on artificial intelligence applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services.Auddia, based in Boulder, Colorado, is recognized for its innovative audio technology that leverages a proprietary AI platform to enhance consumer engagement with AM/FM radio, podcasts, and other audio content. Its flagship product, the 'faidr' superapp, aims to modernize music consumption and promotion by offering ad-free listening, content and ad-break skipping, and integrated artist discovery.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF $3.7B CALIFORNIA TOWER

McCarthy Holdings has reached a significant construction milestone with the topping out of the $3.7 billion California Tower on February 6, 2026. This achievement comes a year and a half after breaking ground on the ambitious project in 2024, as reported by UC Davis Health. The California Tower is designed using a progressive design-build delivery method, marking a pivotal moment in the expansion of UC Davis' Sacramento campus.The California Tower is part of an extensive eight-year plan to double the size of the UC Davis campus, which is recognized as the largest health system capital expansion currently underway in the United States. Once completed, the 14-story hospital and five-story pavilion will house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities dedicated to pharmacy and burn care units. Notably, the tower will also introduce approximately 334 additional inpatient beds to enhance patient care capabilities.In a customary celebration, stakeholders in the project gathered to sign the beam, which was ceremonially placed to mark this important milestone. UC Davis Health anticipates the opening of the California Tower in 2030. This state-of-the-art building is the final component of UC Davis Health's Vision 2030 plan, which aims to increase the campus's total building space from 3.6 million square feet to over 7 million square feet.The California Tower is not McCarthy Holdings' only recent achievement in the healthcare sector. The company completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December. While healthcare construction has shown resilience, contributing to overall growth in construction planning at the end of 2025, early indicators from Dodge's first report of 2026 suggest a slowdown in healthcare project planning at the start of the year.As McCarthy Holdings continues to make strides in the healthcare construction sector, the successful topping out of the California Tower signifies an important step in fulfilling the growing demand for healthcare infrastructure. Investors and stakeholders will be closely watching how these developments unfold as the company forges ahead with its ambitious projects.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS ADVANCES SUSTAINABILITY WITH 2024 GHG EMISSIONS REPORT

McCarthy Holdings, one of the largest employee-owned construction firms in the U.S., has released its updated 2024 Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions Report. The report was published on 16th February 2026, and reflects McCarthy's commitment to strengthening its sustainability efforts across its operations and subsidiaries.Developed in partnership with Gravity, a leading enterprise carbon accounting and energy management platform, the report provides a comprehensive view of McCarthy's carbon footprint. It covers Scope 1, Scope 2, and key categories of Scope 3 emissions, with Scopes 1 and 2 having undergone independent auditing and awarded limited assurance. This commitment to transparency and accountability aligns with McCarthy's mission to be the best builder in America.Ian Pope, Senior Director of Sustainability at McCarthy Holdings, emphasized the company's dedication to community building. 'As an employee-owned company, this important effort is in line with our core values and provides our teams with the practical data necessary to meet regulatory requirements,' he stated. McCarthy's sustainability strategy is anchored in three primary priorities: understanding its environmental impact, acting responsibly for its people and communities, and efficiently managing energy consumption.In addition to emissions reporting, McCarthy Holdings integrates responsible practices into its projects. This includes biodiversity initiatives, responsible fuel management, and the use of digital tools and artificial intelligence for smarter project decisions. The company is also focusing on responsible end-of-life management for solar panels through its advanced PV recycling program, partnering with Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA)-approved recyclers to divert waste from landfills and support a circular economy.By leveraging emissions data and operational insights, McCarthy Holdings continues to demonstrate that its role as a builder of communities extends to fostering a more sustainable future. With a commitment to environmental stewardship and community well-being, McCarthy is setting benchmarks in the construction industry for responsible practices.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES JOINS CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP AS HR DIRECTOR

Carly Reiner Caceres has been appointed as the Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, bringing a wealth of experience in people operations and talent strategy. On the 29th of April 2026, Caceres officially began her new role, where she will be instrumental in supporting national campus recruiting initiatives aimed at bolstering early-career talent pipelines for one of the largest building and infrastructure companies in the United States.Prior to her appointment at Clark Construction Group, Caceres served as the Vice President of People Operations at Proper, where she was pivotal in shaping the HR strategy and operational support within a hybrid work environment. Her extensive background includes significant roles at MILLER as the Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture, where she excelled in various capacities including Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ career trajectory showcases her expertise in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding, which will be crucial as Clark Construction Group seeks to attract top talent from universities across the nation. Her previous experience managing campus recruiting strategies at Grant Thornton LLP further solidifies her capabilities in building robust internship programs and enhancing student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, founded in 1906, has established a strong reputation for delivering value across major markets, including education and infrastructure. With a commitment to community strengthening and project excellence, the company is poised to benefit from Caceres' strategic insights and leadership in HR, particularly in nurturing the next generation of professionals in the construction industry.Caceres’ early career involved internships and roles with various organizations, including Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, which helped her build a diverse skill set in communications and external relations. As she embarks on this new chapter at Clark Construction Group, her multifaceted background is expected to contribute significantly to the company's growth and talent development objectives.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS SECURES MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK WITH RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework, enhancing the delivery of repair, maintenance, and refurbishment services to domestic properties across the local authority area. This announcement was made on 11th February 2026.The framework, which is set to run until 2027, encompasses a variety of multi-trade works. This will enable Renfrewshire Council to effectively manage both responsive and planned maintenance services across its housing stock, ensuring that local residents receive prompt and reliable service.Clark Contracts is already an established player in the public sector, holding several framework agreements that include Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. Additionally, the company is involved with the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and various Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks focusing on Public Buildings, New Build Housing, and more.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, expressed enthusiasm about the new appointment, saying, "Building on our strong track record of delivering more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years, we are pleased to have been appointed to Renfrewshire Council’s framework. As a Paisley-based business, we are proud to be supporting the council and delivering refurbishment and maintenance services that directly benefit local communities."The addition of this framework is expected to bolster Clark Contracts' already significant presence in the public sector, as the company continues to collaborate with various councils and institutions, including the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council, to deliver essential services.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, president and CEO of Clark Development Corp. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. "With this agreement, we ensure that it is a good place for everyone to go and with our coordination, we want to be able to maintain that area [The Villages], an area of preference, where people can reside and really be part of the community," Devanadera stated.PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership with CDC, stating, "We are honored to take part in Clark’s growth and with CDC as our partner, we will deliver this development in Clark." PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. The focus on creating integrated lifestyle hubs aligns with the increasing demand for residential options that complement commercial and recreational spaces, positioning Clark as an attractive destination for future developments.Clark Construction Group's proactive approach in securing this project represents a significant step forward in the region's economic landscape, potentially catalyzing further investments and developments. As the construction timeline unfolds, stakeholders will be keenly observing how this initiative contributes to the overall growth and vibrancy of The Villages and the Clark Freeport Zone.

value.Title: ZACHRY GROUP - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND INDUSTRY PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHW-2903-S1WF-02YP-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION JOINS FORCES WITH TRAYLOR BROS. FOR US-70 ROOSEVELT BRIDGE UPGRADE IN OKLAHOMA

Zachry Construction Corporation, in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc., has secured a contract to replace the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge in Oklahoma, marking a strategic expansion into the state. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry-Traylor JV to lead the project, the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the OklahomaDOT.The announcement, made on 28th April 2026, highlights a major upgrade to a corridor connecting Bryan and Marshall counties and serving Lake Texoma travelers. The existing Roosevelt Bridge has operated since 1945 and remains a critical link for local communities and businesses.The plan calls for a four-lane crossing with bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting, with construction staged to demolish the existing bridge while keeping traffic on the bridge during the work to minimize disruption.The project team combines Zachry's long history in heavy and highway construction with Traylor Bros.' proficiency in complex bridge projects, positioning the teams to deliver a modern crossing that maintains mobility during construction and supports regional growth.Completion is targeted for 1st October 2029, reflecting a broader effort to bolster safety, reliability, and resilience in the region and to support local commerce and tourism.

+ TRAVIS MROSS ELEVATED TO PRESIDENT AT ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION, SIGNALING STRATEGIC TRANSFORMATION

Travis Mross has been named president of Zachry Construction Corp., with his official start in the role on 29th April 2026. The move marks a formal step in the leadership transition at the San Antonio-based, family-owned contractor, where Mross previously served as executive vice president for six years.Since joining the firm as an intern in 2000, Mross has played a central role in shaping the company's strategic direction and bolstering operational efficiency, according to company leaders.He succeeds David Zachry, who will continue to serve as CEO. David Zachry said, "Travis has been instrumental in setting our course and building the strong trajectory we are on today," underscoring Mross's deep understanding of the business and his ability to instill discipline.Zachry Construction is recognized for heavy civil, roadway and infrastructure work, with more than 6,000 completed initiatives across public and private sectors. The company is expanding its water and wastewater infrastructure business, a fast-growing segment, and recently acquired Crescent Constructors Inc. to broaden its capabilities in that arena.As president, Mross will oversee operations across water, transportation, underground utilities and civil sitework. His priorities are strategic growth, operational excellence and cultivating a disciplined, high-performance culture. He said, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY PURCHASES 1.2-ACRE PARCEL CLUSTER TO BACK MONARCH SAN ANTONIO OPERATIONS

On Apr. 29, 2026, Zachry Group, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, completed a ground lease with St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church for a parking lot across South Presa Street from the church near La Villita and Hemisfair. In a separate transaction, the group acquired a smaller parking lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, expanding the total area to about 1.2 acres. The sites were previously connected to Dennis McDaniel, the Austin developer who had planned an apartment complex there before the pandemic disrupted that plan, a larger project originally envisaged with Opportunity Home San Antonio for 252 apartments and retail space.A Zachry spokesperson said the parking assets will support operational needs for the Monarch San Antonio hotel, which opened recently overlooking Civic Park. There are no plans for further development on these parcels, underscoring a focus on hospitality operations rather than new construction.Zachry Group has deep ties to San Antonio, dating to its founding in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, and its work on the Monarch project underscores the firm's continued role in the city’s growth. The Monarch hotel project is valued at roughly $185 million and is a flagship example of Zachry's involvement in both construction and hospitality in the region.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ BECHTEL PARTNERS WITH SOWELA TO STRENGTHEN LOCAL PIPEFITTING SKILLS PIPELINE

Bechtel and SOWELA Technical Community College are launching a no-cost pipefitting training program at SOWELA's Main Campus in Lake Charles, designed to build a local pipeline of skilled workers for manufacturing, energy, and building systems. The program runs from 26th May 2026 through 13th November 2026.The program provides an introduction to the pipefitting trade, including core responsibilities, safety principles, and the tools and materials commonly used on the job. Trainees will gain hands-on experience with high- and low-pressure piping systems that support plants, facilities, and infrastructure.Classes meet Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m., with completion yielding an OSHA 10-hour safety credential. Participants will be prepared to pursue NCCER Pipefitting certifications, which can boost employability in a competitive local job market.To be eligible for employment consideration, completers must pass a background check and drug screening. Interested residents can learn more or register at www.sowela.edu/pipefitting, and can reach SOWELA's Office of Workforce Solutions at (337) 421-6560 or workforce@sowela.edu for questions.The Bechtel-SOWELA collaboration underscores a shared commitment to workforce development in Lake Charles, addressing rising demand for skilled labor in construction and manufacturing and supporting regional economic growth.

+ UNC CHARLOTTE LAUNCHES ALBERT SCHOOL OF CONSTRUCTION WITH MAJOR BECHTEL GIFT

UNC Charlotte alumni Craig and Darla Albert have given a leadership gift to establish the Albert School of Construction within the William States Lee College of Engineering, launching a new model for construction education in one of the nation ’s fastest-growing markets. The announcement was made Apr. 7, 2026. The school will focus on the full capital project lifecycle, training students to lead from planning and engineering through delivery of complex infrastructure. Chancellor Sharon L. Gaber noted that the investment helps align education with regional and national needs. Bechtel Group’s Craig Albert underscored the industry’s demand for skilled workers, citing a real shortage of construction capacity.

+ LUCY MARTIN TO LEAD BECHTEL’S MINING & METALS AS AILIE MACADAM RETIRES

Bechtel named Lucy Martin as president of its Mining & Metals (M&M) business, effective April 6, 2026, as Ailie MacAdam prepares to retire in May after a 41-year Bechtel career. Craig Albert, Bechtel President and COO, stated on 3rd April 2026, "Lucy is a proven leader with the strategic vision, deep mining sector knowledge, operational discipline, and customer focus to advance our Mining & Metals business." Martin has spent over two decades at Bechtel, where she has developed high-performing global teams aimed at solving complex challenges for customers across various markets. In her new role, Martin said she intends to build on Bechtel's momentum in mining, acknowledging a rapidly evolving global market. "Our focus will be on enhancing how we deliver results for our customers, particularly in areas such as cost-effective development, productivity, safety, reliability, and certainty of outcomes," she added. MacAdam's tenure delivered major project progress in key markets and led to the highest new-work bookings in 15 years for Mining & Metals in 2025. Bechtel said it remains a trusted partner across sectors including Energy, Infrastructure, Manufacturing & Technology, Mining & Metals, and Nuclear, and will continue to address the demands of a changing energy landscape under its new leader.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT-AUSTIN COMMERCIAL JV TO DELIVER OPERATIONS & MAINTENANCE FACILITY FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL

On 28th April 2026, a joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial secured a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line, which will span 15 stations along a 10-mile corridor and feature all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes through much of the day.Kiewit and Austin Commercial will design and build the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched to ensure reliable daily service.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, contributing to the overall functionality of the system.The project includes about 10 design-stage subcontractors and is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally said, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally added that partnering with Kiewit, a world-class firm with deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success and broader economic opportunities across Central Texas. The LINC Austin program, initially led by Aecom, includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting among its delivery-partner collaborators.

+ AUSTIN TRANSIT PARTNERSHIP NAMES KAP TO LEAD OMF FOR PROJECT CONNECT

The Austin Transit Partnership's board has chosen Kiewit Austin Partnership, a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial, as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility, enabling immediate pre-construction activities such as design advancement, permitting, and site preparation. The decision was announced on 21st April 2026.The OMF footprint covers about 62 acres on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles. To make room for the facility, property acquisitions will affect dozens of existing businesses, with relocation concerns centered on Airport Commerce Park.KAP currently brings roughly 10 subcontractors into the design phase, with more work expected as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. The OMF is expected to support about 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, contributing to local economic growth. Mainline construction is expected to start in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration published its final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision in January 2026, allowing the project to move into final design and land acquisition.

+ KIEWIT TO LEAD PRE-FEED FOR ATERNIUM'S $1 BILLION HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PLANT

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility, a step in its roughly $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site is designed to meet rising demand for low-carbon energy and strengthen domestic capabilities for heavy water, which is currently sourced primarily from countries such as China and Iran. Andrew Cottone, founder and CEO of Aternium, noted that the collaboration with Kiewit aligns with the company's emphasis on technical excellence and safety standards.During the pre-FEED phase, Kiewit will help define the project scope, optimize design parameters, and lay a solid foundation for future development, according to the companies. Tyler Nordquist, executive vice president at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., said the partnership will add substantial value in this early stage.Aternium's integrated production approach combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water, a dual output designed to lower hydrogen production costs and to address vulnerabilities in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. The project sits at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing and aims to deliver scalable benefits to local communities through workforce development and job creation.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION TO GROW INNSBROOK FOOTPRINT WITH 9,100-SF EXPANSION BACKED BY HENRICO COUNTY

DPR Construction announced a 9,100-square-foot expansion of its Innsbrook office, supported by Henrico County incentives to attract and retain businesses. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion aims to accommodate a growing workforce and strengthen operational capabilities as part of the company’s regional growth strategy.The project will bring together teams and resources, promoting collaboration and innovation across the organization.Sustainable design features and modern office concepts will be integrated, aligning with DPR’s leadership in sustainable construction.Completion is expected within the next year, underscoring the company’s commitment to the region and its ongoing community initiatives.

+ JASON PAGE APPOINTED HEAD OF DPR CONSTRUCTION'S NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

The Special Services Group handles a broad range of work, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces, and Page's leadership is expected to expand the group's capabilities to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs in Nashville. Beyond project leadership, Page mentors and coaches teams, sharing best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work and fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation as DPR expands its Nashville footprint.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SECURES GREAT PLACE TO WORK STATUS AS STAFF REPORT HIGH ENGAGEMENT

DPR Construction has secured Great Place To Work Certification, confirming that 97 percent of its employees say it is a great place to work, a figure about 40 points higher than the national average. The award, presented on 31st March 2026, signals a culture rooted in pride, belonging and opportunity across the firm’s projects.CEO George Pfeffer noted the certification reflects sentiments across the organization. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said. The company emphasizes trust, support, and growth to help employees perform at their best.DPR’s operating model empowers staff to make decisions and share information openly, encouraging initiative and cross functional collaboration on complex tasks, new roles, and ideas beyond job descriptions. A strong emphasis on learning and development supports clear career paths aligned with individual aspirations.Great Place To Work® research aligns with these findings, highlighting a sense of pride and belonging among workers. The certification has helped DPR strengthen its reputation as an employer of choice, while external accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek have recognized DPR in more than 15 U.S. cities over the last year.Founded in 1990, DPR has evolved into a multi billion dollar contractor known for complex and sustainable projects across sectors. Its innovation posture—through virtual design and construction and self performing craft—helps explain its roughly 11,000 person workforce worldwide. More information about DPR’s culture and career opportunities is available on its official website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) JOINS WITH EVERYWHERE COMMUNICATIONS TO DEVELOP RESILIENT AUTONOMOUS DRONE PLATFORM

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) and EVERYWHERE Communications forged a strategic partnership to accelerate autonomous drone development under the SBIR initiative, targeting operations in degraded and disconnected environments. The partnership was confirmed on 4th of May 2026. The collaboration leverages Iridium Satellite technology to deliver a resilient data transport layer that lets drones operate autonomously while streaming essential sensor data back to operators and command systems. The system supports beyond-line-of-sight operations, reliable data exfiltration and autonomous mission execution, with scalable coordination for fleets of drones on low-bandwidth links. Jake Bailey, President of EVERYWHERE Communications, notes, "In austere environments, connectivity is never guaranteed. We're enabling drones to keep operating—and keep delivering intelligence—even when the network is compromised." Parsons contributes TAK-as-a-Service (TaaS) to provide scalable, mission-ready integration and sustainment services tailored for the Department of War and federal agencies, enhancing real-time situational awareness and data interoperability. Mike Kushin, President of Defense and Intelligence at Parsons, adds, "In an era where every second and every signal matters, this collaboration brings together resilient autonomy in the air and trusted mission systems on the ground to give our warfighters and intelligence professionals a decisive information advantage." The alliance seeks to redefine operational capabilities for mission-critical teams worldwide.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE: PSN) SETS DELEGATED PROGRAM MANAGER ROLE FOR THE BORING COMPANY'S DUBAI LOOP

CHANTILLY, Va. — The Boring Company awards Parsons a nine-month contract for the Dubai Loop project, dated 5th May 2026Parsons will serve as TBC’s delegated program manager, overseeing design-build activities for the Dubai Loop project and delivering Independent Design Verification, Stakeholder Management, and Permitting and NOC Support for the pilot phase. The firm will also perform multidisciplinary reviews of designs across civil, structural, mechanical, electrical, safety, and utility disciplinesPierre Santoni, president of Infrastructure EMEA at Parsons, highlighted the UAE's ongoing infrastructure portfolio and stated, "Parsons has proudly played a central role in developing some of the most important and prestigious critical infrastructure programs across the UAE since our first programs there in the 1980s." He added that the partnership with TBC aligns with the UAE leadership's vision to foster strategic partnerships and enhance advanced technologies as part of the Dubai 2040 Urban Master PlanThe Dubai Loop project, developed in collaboration with Dubai’s Roads and Transit Authority (RTA), aims to expand the city’s transport ecosystem through a series of passenger transport tunnels and stations. Parsons has a history of working with the RTA, having contributed to significant transportation infrastructure projects in the Emirate since 2005, including the Dubai Metro and various highway and bridge projectsParsons Corporation has managed over 250 complex tunneling projects globally and has extensive experience in road and highway development, covering more than 10,000 miles across six continents. As a provider of disruptive technologies in national security and global infrastructure, Parsons is well-positioned to contribute to the Dubai Loop initiative, enhancing urban mobility and transportation efficiency in one of the world's most dynamic cities

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) WINS NYC DEP PROJECT FOR LARGE-DIAMETER STORAGE TUNNEL AND DEWATERING PUMP STATION AND SECURES USCG PROGRAM MANAGEMENT CONTRACT VIA GSI AMERICAS JV

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) Posts Progress On Apr. 14, 2026 Contract Award To Lead The Construction Of The Newtown Creek CSO Storage Tunnel In New York City. The Project Features A Large-Diameter Storage Tunnel And A Dewatering Pump Station To Curb Untreated DischargesThe New York City Department Of Environmental Protection Has Entrusted Parsons With This Critical Task, Citing The Firm's Experience On More Than 250 Tunnel And Ocean Outfall Projects Worldwide. The Initiative Aims To Modernize Municipal Assets, Improve Water Quality, And Support Urban Sustainability Through A Technology-Driven ApproachIn Addition, Parsons Forms A Joint Venture With GSI Americas That Secures A US Coast Guard Program Management Contract Worth $25 Million. The Contract Supports The USCG Execution Plan, Including Significant Upgrades To Shore Infrastructure And Deployment Of Transformational TechnologiesThe Partnership Highlights Parsons' Strategic Intent To Assist Federal Agencies In Securing And Modernizing Critical Digital And Physical Assets. The Arrangement Aligns With The Company's Broader Focus On Defense And Cybersecurity Markets And Reflects Its Ability To Deliver Integrated Solutions For Urban And National Security ChallengesInvestors May Monitor The Company's Ongoing Growth Across Infrastructure And Defense Initiatives As Parsons Expands Its Portfolio

value.Title: Texas Energy Fund produces first plant

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ0-DD93-RW1S-R0N0-00000-00Texas Energy Fund produces first plant2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:34ZLexisNexisCPg. C001Texas Energy Fund produces first plantClaire HaoStaff Writer

1) PHOTO: Gov. Greg Abbott speaks at a news conference on Tuesday, when he announced that the first project supported by the Texas Energy Fund, Constellation's Pin Oak Creek power plant, has come online in Fairfield. (Gov. Greg Abbott's office)

The Texas Energy Fund, a controversial $10 billion taxpayer-backed program, has officially produced its first natural gas power plant for the state's power grid after some initial hiccups.

The power plant, dubbed Pin Oak Creek, was announced in 2023 by Houston-based Calpine, a company that has since been acquired by Baltimore-based Constellation. The facility is located in Freestone County in central Texas and adds 460 megawatts of electricity generation, enough to power approximately 115,000 homes during the hottest summer hours.

NRG Energy, another Houston-based power generation company, expects to bring online one of its Texas Energy Fund-backed power plants located in Houston later this summer.

At a Tuesday news conference, Gov. Greg Abbott said the Pin Oak Creek power plant coming online was "a tremendous day for the state of Texas."

"There's no reason why the Texas power grid will ever run out of power again," Abbott said, referencing the 2021 winter storm that knocked out all kinds of power plants and triggered mass outages across the state.

Most of the Texas Energy Fund is meant to support up to 10,000 megawatts of natural gas power plants through grants and low-interest loans. Pin Oak Creek is the first project receiving such a loan to start operations, nearly three years after voters approved the program.

In that time, the fund has faced a number of project dropouts, messy fraud allegations and withholding of public records, as well as criticism that it's not needed or too slow in bringing projects online.

"It might come with a name that sounds nice and beneficial," advocacy group Public Citizen wrote last year of the Texas Energy Fund. "Still, the details tell a different story, one of a state government intent on using tax dollars to prop up the fossil fuel industry in its losing battle against renewable energy."

Through it all, Texas politicians have championed the program as critical in achieving one of their key energy priorities: encouraging the build-out of more natural gas power plants.

In recent years, most of the new power supply that's been added to the state's electric grid has been solar arrays and battery storage, which push down electricity prices and make it less economic to build fossil fuel power plants.

At various points, Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick has called the Texas Energy Fund his "baby" and threatened that the state would build its own power plants if the private sector didn't step up.

In his Tuesday remarks, Abbott cheered Texas as "America's undisputed power leader" -- number one in oil and gas production, wind power generation, utility-scale solar power and utility-scale battery storage.

Natural gas power plants like Pin Oak Creek, however, are still needed to ensure there's plentiful electricity regardless of sun or wind availability, Abbott said.

"With projects like this, it doesn't matter what time of the day it is, Texas is going to have plenty of power to make sure that power remains on for every household, every business across the state of Texas," Abbott said.

Calpine CEO Andrew Novotny said the company originally bought the turbines in the Pin Oak Creek plant more than 10 years ago, "but the market at the time didn't need us back then." So, it waited until market conditions changed a few years ago, as electricity demand in Texas began surging from economic and population growth.

"Pin Oak Creek is designed to operate when demand is at its highest and reliability matters the most. It's not meant to run all the time, and it's also not built to serve just a single (electricity user)," Novotny said.

David Dardis, chief external affairs and growth officer for Constellation, said increasing the company's capabilities in the fast-growing Texas market was why it decided to acquire Calpine, previously Houston's largest private company.

"When Constellation first envisioned combining with Calpine, it was projects like the Pin Oak Creek facility here in Texas that was a key part of the strategic rationale for the transaction," he said.

value.Title: DTE Energy Company 's 10-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms American Electric Power Company, Inc.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-DPW3-RXW9-W11G-00000-00DTE Energy Company's 10-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms American Electric Power Company, Inc.2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:34ZLexisNexisDTE Energy Company's 10-Year Earnings Growth Outperforms American Electric Power Company, Inc.

NYSE STOCK REPORT

DTE Energy Company's growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 7.4% annualized over 10 years.

In comparison, American Electric Power Company, Inc.'s growth in EPS (earnings per share) has been 4.9% annualized over 10 years.

DTE Energy Company's last trading price was $142.44 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 22.1. With an EPS growth rate of 8.4 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 2.63 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

American Electric Power Company, Inc.'s last trading price was $132.56 which implies a Price to Earnings multiple of 19.8. With an EPS growth rate of 14.1 that implies a PEG ratio (price/earnings to growth) of 1.4 when using the EPS growth rate of the past 3 years (investors prefer using a 3-year expected growth rate instead but each investor will have a different perspective on future growth).

Company Profile

DTE Energy operates as a Detroit-based diversified energy enterprise involved in the development and management of energy-related businesses and services nationwide. The core business provides vital energy infrastructure, with its largest revenue sources driven by regulated utility operations. These utility operations include an electric company serving 2.3 million customers in Southeast Michigan and a natural gas company serving 1.4 million customers across the state. The fastest-growing segments feature major data center expansions and custom energy solutions, which drive incremental infrastructure investments. Furthermore, the company maintains non-utility energy businesses focused on renewable energy generation and energy marketing and trading. By accelerating the transition to cleaner energy generation, the firm continually delivers reliable and sustainable power to residential, commercial, and large industrial end markets across the region.

Company Profile

American Electric Power is primarily engaged in the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity across a diversified service territory spanning the eastern and western United States. The company serves various retail and wholesale customers operating in both competitive and regulated end markets. Its largest revenue sources are the Vertically Integrated Utilities segment, contributing $462 million in earnings, and the Transmission and Distribution Utilities segment, generating $237 million. The fastest-growing areas include transmission assets under AEP Transmission Holdco, driven by new PJM and SPP project awards, and commercial and industrial sales volumes bolstered by expanding data-processing load additions in Ohio and Texas. Overall, the business benefits from constructive regulatory environments that support capital investments while effectively addressing the rapidly rising demand from large-load commercial end-use customers.

value.Title: April 28, 2026: Swinerton Chosen as CM/GC for $145 Million SF Fire Department Training Facility

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-KD23-RXGW-R1K7-00000-00April 28, 2026: Swinerton Chosen as CM/GC for $145 Million SF Fire Department Training Facility2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Swinerton Chosen as CM/GC for $145 Million SF Fire Department Training Facility

Swinerton Builders has been selected as the Construction Manager/General Contractor for a $145 million project to build the San Francisco Fire Department’s Division of Training facility. The contract, awarded by San Francisco Public Works, marks a major step in updating the city’s public safety training infrastructure. Groundbreaking occurred on 16th April 2026 in the southeastern district of San Francisco.

The campus will feature a 50,000-square-foot scenario district designed to mirror San Francisco neighborhoods, with training environments such as sloped intersections, narrow hallways, and structures that simulate post-earthquake conditions to give recruits and veterans realistic scenarios. The project will also support emergency medical service training, onsite fire apparatus training, and Neighborhood Emergency Response Team sessions.

An Administration/Classroom Building will be designated as an Essential Services Building to ensure immediate occupancy after a major earthquake and will serve as a backup emergency operations center. Swinerton says the project reflects its ongoing commitment to safety, resilience, and collaboration with city stakeholders. The company brings a 135-year legacy and extensive experience in San Francisco, including more than 40 public safety facilities.

INDEX

SECTION 1 SWINERTON INCORPORATED PROFILE

SECTION 2 SWINERTON INCORPORATED TOP MANAGEMENT

SECTION 3 SWINERTON INCORPORATED RANKING

SECTION 1 SWINERTON INCORPORATED PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

value.Title: Tesla's 2026 Organizational Structure: A Look Inside Musk's Vision

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHM-DPW3-RXP3-K38M-00000-00Tesla's 2026 Organizational Structure: A Look Inside Musk's Vision2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:34ZLexisNexisTesla's 2026 Organizational Structure: A Look Inside Musk's Vision

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

06 May 2026 12:03 EDT

Tesla, Inc. continues to redefine the automotive landscape with its unique organizational structure, reflecting the visionary leadership of Elon Musk. As of May 5th, 2026, the company is characterized by a functional organizational model that integrates hierarchical elements, creating a framework designed for innovation and rapid decision-making.

At Tesla's headquarters in Austin, Texas, centralized decision-making authority is concentrated in the hands of executive leadership, including Musk himself. This structure supports various functional centers that encompass critical business activities such as finance, sales, marketing, engineering, and design, enabling coordinated operations across multiple product lines. The company’s hierarchical nature facilitates quick innovation cycles, essential for maintaining its competitive edge in the electric vehicle market.

Comparatively, Tesla's organizational structure diverges from traditional automotive companies like Ford and General Motors. While Ford employs a decentralized approach allowing regional managers some autonomy, Tesla maintains a more centralized framework that prioritizes consistency in strategy and execution. This can enhance operational efficiency but may limit responsiveness to local market nuances. Notably, Tesla's structure is not strictly hierarchical; it reflects Musk's preference for minimal bureaucracy, enabling fluid communication between departments. Employees can easily collaborate, often bypassing formal channels to foster innovation.

The implications of Tesla's organizational design are significant, particularly concerning market responsiveness and brand consistency. While the centralized model accelerates decision-making, it may hinder adaptability in diverse markets. In contrast, Toyota's hybrid structure offers a balance between central control and local adaptability, highlighting the trade-offs inherent in Tesla's approach. Nonetheless, the strong focus on innovation has allowed Tesla to remain at the forefront of the automotive and energy sectors, particularly as the company integrates AI-driven initiatives into its operational framework.

In conclusion, Tesla's 2026 organizational structure underscores the profound influence of Elon Musk on the company's strategic direction. With a functional hierarchy that emphasizes collaboration and minimal bureaucracy, Tesla aims to maintain its position as a leader in electric vehicles and renewable energy solutions. As the automotive landscape evolves, understanding this structure will be crucial for investors and industry observers alike.

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: April 28, 2026: Swinerton Secures Key Role in Building SF Fire Department’s Division of Training, a $145 Million Project

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHK-F5B3-RRV5-42RN-00000-00April 28, 2026: Swinerton Secures Key Role in Building SF Fire Department’s Division of Training, a $145 Million Project2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Swinerton Secures Key Role in Building SF Fire Department’s Division of Training, a $145 Million Project

Swinerton Builders has been selected as the Construction Manager/General Contractor for a $145 million project to build the San Francisco Fire Department’s Division of Training facility, a contract awarded by San Francisco Public Works. Groundbreaking occurred on 16th April 2026.

Located in the city's southeastern district, the campus will include a 50,000-square-foot scenario district that recreates San Francisco neighborhoods with realistic urban training environments, including sloped intersections and cramped spaces.

In addition to the scenario district, the center will offer emergency medical service training, on-site fire apparatus training, and Neighborhood Emergency Response Team sessions. The Administration/Classroom Building will be an Essential Services Building enabling rapid use after a major earthquake and functioning as a backup EOC.

Christina Pantera, project executive at Swinerton, pointed to the firm's experience with more than 250 San Francisco projects, including over 40 public safety facilities, and said the collaboration with city stakeholders will guide the design. Swinerton brings a 135-year legacy of safety, innovation, and community service to the project.

INDEX

SECTION 1 SWINERTON INCORPORATED PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

1.2 SUMMARY

Employees: About 900

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHJ-MBG3-S5YM-F1M3-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:01:26ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ SWINERTON CHOSEN FOR GROUNDBREAKING FIRE DEPARTMENT TRAINING FACILITY

Swinerton Builders has been selected as the Construction Manager/General Contractor for a new $145 million training facility for the San Francisco Fire Department (SFFD). This significant contract, awarded by San Francisco Public Works, is aimed at enhancing public safety and emergency response capabilities in the Bay Area. The groundbreaking for the facility took place on 16th April 2026, marking a pivotal step in modernizing the training infrastructure for the city's first responders.Located in the southeastern district of San Francisco, the new SFFD Division of Training campus will feature a 50,000-square-foot scenario district designed to replicate the unique characteristics of San Francisco neighborhoods. The facility will include various training environments, such as sloped intersections, narrow hallways, and structures that simulate post-earthquake conditions, enabling recruits and veterans alike to experience realistic emergency scenarios. Paul Hinz, vice president and division manager of Swinerton's San Francisco/East Bay division, emphasized the importance of this center in equipping first responders with essential training resources.In addition to the scenario district, the state-of-the-art center will host emergency medical service training, onsite fire apparatus training, and community-based Neighborhood Emergency Response Team (NERT) training sessions. The Administration/Classroom Building will be designated as an Essential Services Building, ensuring immediate occupancy after a major earthquake and serving as a backup emergency operations center. This multi-faceted approach reflects Swinerton's commitment to enhancing community resilience and preparedness.Christina Pantera, project executive at Swinerton, highlighted the collaborative nature of the project, noting the company's extensive experience with over 250 projects in San Francisco, including more than 40 public safety facilities. She reaffirmed Swinerton's dedication to working closely with city stakeholders and community members to deliver a facility tailored to the needs of San Francisco's emergency services. The company's hands-on approach will involve its skilled craft employees executing key construction elements, ensuring high standards of quality and safety.With a legacy spanning over 135 years, Swinerton Builders is well-positioned to lead this transformative project. Their expertise in building public service infrastructure underscores a deep commitment to safety, innovation, and collaboration. As the construction of the SFFD Division of Training facility progresses, it promises to serve as a critical asset to the San Francisco Fire Department, enhancing the city's readiness to respond to emergencies and safeguarding the community for years to come.

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS MISSION CRITICAL TEAM WITH KRISTEN BEJARANO APPOINTMENT

Swinerton Incorporated has announced the appointment of Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical. This strategic move, revealed on the 8th of April, 2026, underscores Swinerton's commitment to enhancing its capabilities in the burgeoning mission critical and data center infrastructure market.Bejarano brings nearly 25 years of extensive experience in delivering complex, high-demand projects, having made significant contributions to the mission critical construction landscape for some of the largest global technology firms. Her notable achievements include launching and scaling a national mission critical and data center construction division, and managing the strategy and procurement for a major technology company's Americas data center program.In her new role at Swinerton, Bejarano will spearhead the company's mission critical strategy, collaborating with clients to translate intricate technical requirements into actionable plans that emphasize speed-to-market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. Chris Chany, Chief Revenue Officer of Swinerton, expressed confidence in Bejarano's ability to significantly enhance the growth of the mission critical sector while also improving client experiences.Bejarano's passion for the evolving landscape of mission critical engineering, particularly in areas such as cooling technologies and renewable energy integration, positions her as a key leader in delivering innovative solutions. Her leadership is expected to enable Swinerton to address the growing demands of the market while maintaining a strong focus on sustainability and operational efficiency.Swinerton Incorporated, founded in 1888 and wholly employee-owned, comprises Swinerton Builders, Timberlab, Inc., SAK Builders, Inc., and Lindgren Development Inc. It provides a range of construction services across the United States, with a commitment to quality and innovation in every project.

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS NORTHEAST DIVISION WITH NEW LEADERSHIP

Swinerton Builders, an industry-leading commercial general contractor with offices nationwide, has announced a significant leadership transition in its Northeast Division. This promotion of Jay Quackenbush to vice president and division manager is part of a broader strategy to bolster the firm’s presence in the growing Northeast construction market. Following this move, which took effect on 16th March 2026, the Northeast Division has also welcomed Nicholas Schifferle as director of client relations and Robert Murphy as project executive.Jay Quackenbush, a long-time New York native, brings nearly three decades of experience overseeing complex construction projects to his new role. His deep understanding of New York City's unique building conditions and extensive industry relationships position him well to drive the division's strategic goals. Ray Haj, Swinerton's chief operational officer and East Coast regional manager, emphasized that Quackenbush's leadership will enhance the team's capacity to meet client needs, ranging from facility management to large-scale ground-up projects. Furthermore, Quackenbush is actively involved in community engagement, serving on several boards and partnering with local nonprofits.Nicholas Schifferle, who joined Swinerton in 2025, adds more than 20 years of international business development experience to the team. His background includes managing high-profile design and construction projects across Asia and Australia, as well as founding a design-build firm in San Francisco. Quackenbush noted Schifferle's ability to adapt to rapid industry changes and his people-focused approach, which has made him a valuable asset to both the team and its clients. Schifferle's longstanding involvement with CoreNet Global reflects his commitment to advancing the commercial real estate sector.Meanwhile, Robert Murphy, with his extensive portfolio of overseeing over $650 million in commercial interiors construction, is tasked with leading multiple project teams to ensure quality and client satisfaction. Quackenbush praised Murphy's operational capabilities and his ability to elevate team performance. Together with long-standing division leaders Sloane Brown and Peter Fontana, Murphy will work to grow Swinerton’s footprint in the Northeast's commercial interiors market.Since its establishment in the Northeast in 2021, Swinerton's division has delivered a variety of significant projects, including office fit-outs, historic renovations, and ground-up constructions across all five boroughs. With a robust project pipeline and a commitment to excellence, the team continues to expand its portfolio into education, residential, and public sectors. Quackenbush affirmed that the division is well-positioned for future growth, driven by a talented team dedicated to serving the communities in which they operate.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD AVIATION OPERATIONS IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations in the Carolinas, effective immediately. Johnson's new role will see him leading and expanding the company’s aviation sector across North and South Carolina, building on a career that spans over 19 years in the construction industry.As of 1st April 2026, Trent Johnson’s promotion is a reflection of his extensive experience, including more than 12 and a half years with Balfour Beatty. During his tenure, he has successfully contributed to the delivery of 22 aviation projects, ranging from targeted renovations to complex, multi-phase programmes. His leadership has been pivotal in enhancing Balfour Beatty’s aviation presence, notably at Raleigh-Durham International Airport (RDU) and Albert J. Ellis Airport.In his new capacity, Johnson will continue to support Balfour Beatty’s existing aviation clients while expanding the company’s footprint at additional regional and international airports throughout the Carolinas. His focus will remain on assisting project teams in navigating the complexities of active airport environments, while prioritizing safety, operational continuity, and stakeholder coordination.Jeff Sandeen, President of Balfour Beatty in the Carolinas, commented on Johnson’s promotion, stating, “Trent has built a strong reputation as a trusted leader and client partner within our local aviation operations. His ability to guide teams through the complexities of active airport environments has been a key driver of our success in the region.” Sandeen expressed confidence that Johnson will play a crucial role in shaping the next phase of growth for Balfour Beatty’s aviation portfolio.Trent Johnson holds a degree in Construction Engineering Management from North Carolina State University and is also known for his community involvement, particularly with the Make-A-Wish Foundation. His steady leadership style and collaborative approach have helped Balfour Beatty deliver projects that align with long-term regional growth and infrastructure priorities.

+ MAINMARK UK JOINS BALFOUR BEATTY'S CP7 FRAMEWORK FOR RAIL INFRASTRUCTURE

Ground engineering specialist Mainmark UK has secured a place on the Balfour Beatty plc CP7 Framework, positioning the company to support the delivery of key rail infrastructure works across Network Rail’s latest five-year investment programme. This investment period, known as Control Period 7 (CP7), spans from 1st April 2024 to 31st March 2029, with an allocation of approximately £43–45 billion across Great Britain.The CP7 programme focuses on maintaining, renewing, and modernising the rail network to deliver a safer, more reliable, and more sustainable railway. Investment priorities during CP7 include infrastructure maintenance and renewals, digital signalling and technology, safety improvements, and initiatives aimed at enhancing both freight and passenger performance across the network.As part of the CP7 Framework, contractors will engage in a variety of works aligned with Network Rail’s Strategic Business Plan. These activities will encompass low-complexity emergency callouts, planned preventative maintenance, and small-scale civil engineering schemes. Mainmark UK will be involved in geotechnical response works, bridge repairs, structural integrity projects, platform refurbishments, and drainage clearance, among others.Tom Kavanagh, General Manager at Mainmark UK, expressed enthusiasm about the collaboration with Balfour Beatty, stating, “We’re looking forward to collaborating with Balfour Beatty to help deliver critical infrastructure works across the network during the CP7 period. Being selected to support Balfour Beatty on this framework is a fantastic opportunity for our team and aligns perfectly with our growth plans.”Mainmark is known for its innovative ground engineering solutions, particularly its Teretek® resin injection technology, which stabilises weak ground conditions and fills subsurface voids without extensive excavation. This technology is crucial for maintaining the integrity of rail infrastructure, especially as heavier freight loads and severe weather events put increasing pressure on railway assets. With its appointment to the CP7 Framework, Mainmark UK is set to play a vital role in ensuring the resilience and reliability of the UK's rail network.

+ BALFOUR BEATTY'S GROWTH FUELED BY RECORD ORDER BOOK

Balfour Beatty PLC has reported impressive growth, driven by its robust performance in the construction sector, particularly in power transmission projects. The company’s market valuation has soared nearly two-thirds over the past year, a noteworthy achievement for a FTSE 250 constituent. This growth trajectory is largely attributed to a record order book and a 9 percent increase in underlying earnings per share, which reached 47.6 pence.On 11th March 2026, the construction group revealed that its top-line growth was significantly supported by the increasing demand for power transmission in the UK and a surge in building projects in the US. Operating profits within Balfour Beatty's support services segment saw a remarkable increase of one-third, amounting to £122 million, alongside an 80 basis point rise in the underlying margin to 8.5 percent. The company’s UK construction division achieved its margin targets ahead of schedule, underscoring its operational efficiency.In the US market, Balfour Beatty has capitalized on rising building volumes although it faced challenges due to cost overruns in a project within its Civils arm. The dual market strategy appears to be paying off, as the company navigates through these hurdles while maintaining a focus on profitability. The expansion of infrastructure projects, particularly in power transmission, has provided a solid foundation for growth, enabling Balfour Beatty to solidify its position in the competitive construction landscape.Market analysts remain optimistic about Balfour Beatty’s future prospects, considering its substantial order book which continues to grow amidst a backdrop of increasing infrastructure investment. The company’s strategic initiatives to enhance operational margins and deliver projects on time have positioned it well for sustained growth in the coming quarters. Investors are likely to monitor Balfour Beatty’s performance closely as it seeks to leverage its strengths in both the UK and US markets.As Balfour Beatty continues to capitalize on the opportunities presented by the expanding power grid and overall construction demand, it remains crucial for stakeholders to assess the potential impacts of rising costs and project management challenges. Overall, the company’s recent performance indicates a strong recovery trajectory, making it a noteworthy player in the construction sector as it heads into the next fiscal year.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ HENSEL PHELPS CONSTRUCTION CO [3 News That Matters]

+ TRACK3D SECURES MAJOR PARTNERSHIP WITH HENSEL PHELPS FOR CONSTRUCTION TECHNOLOGY

Track3D, an AI-driven construction technology company, has marked a significant milestone by securing a multi-year agreement with Hensel Phelps, expanding its collaboration from a single pilot to the deployment of Track3D’s Reality Intelligence platform across more than 200 active projects. This announcement was made on 1st April 2026, emphasizing Track3D's growing influence in the construction sector.Hensel Phelps plans to standardize the use of Track3D for portfolio-wide progress monitoring, indicating strong confidence in the platform’s scalability and effectiveness across diverse construction projects. By utilizing automated progress tracking, Hensel Phelps aims to identify on-site issues earlier, which can significantly reduce delays and enhance schedule reliability, especially in complex builds.A notable case study from San Francisco International Airport highlighted the successful application of Track3D’s technology on a challenging infrastructure project. Project leaders reported a shift from reactive issue resolution to proactive decision-making, facilitated by 360deg site walks and the involvement of existing field personnel, thereby minimizing the need for major workflow changes. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future.
